prd_summary:
Replace the iframe embed for AgentField build logs with a native logs widget that displays cached logs from SweAfJobEntity.Logs field, with a Refresh button to fetch fresh logs from the control plane API via FetchExecutionDetailAsync, integrated inline with the existing Build detail page layout.

Acceptance Criteria:
- BuildDetail.razor should display a non-iframe logs widget instead of the existing iframe embed at lines 132-149
- The logs widget uses job.Logs field when populated via webhook caching from SweAfService.ApplyEventAsync
- A Refresh button calls FetchExecutionDetailAsync and updates job.Logs with fresh log content from control plane
- The logs display in a scrollable container similar to the existing Build Log tab style
- For builds with no per-job control plane URL, logs are fetched from hub-level control plane via ResolveControlPlaneUrlAsync
- Calling FetchExecutionDetailAsync updates job.Logs field and persists to database via SaveChanges
- The logs widget includes a Copy button that copies logs to clipboard using browser clipboard API
- The logs widget includes a Download button that triggers file download of logs as .log file
architecture_summary: Native Blazor logs widget architecture replacing iframe embed. Uses cached SweAfJobEntity.Logs from webhooks, refresh button fetches fresh logs via FetchExecutionDetailAsync, fallback to hub-level control plane for early failed builds.

all_issues.acceptance_criteria: A Refresh button calls FetchExecutionDetailAsync and updates job.Logs with fresh log content from control plane, then persists to database via SaveChanges.
all_issues.description: The RefreshLogs method in BuildDetail.razor needs to call FetchExecutionDetailAsync to fetch fresh logs from the control plane, update the job.Logs field, and persist changes to the database via SaveChanges.
all_issues.name: build-detail-native-logs-widget
all_issues.title: RefreshLogs method should call FetchExecutionDetailAsync and persist to database
